spec: Task 0027 is top of Now and signed off (frontmatter: APPROVED 2026-07-09), but its full plan — new noema/substrate/operators.py with 5 ported EoH templates + provenance banners, controller changes (operator choice, arity-2 parent draw, thought-extraction parse, checkpoint RNG round-trip), NoemaConfig fields/validators, prompts.py passthrough, a new test file plus ~6 additions to test_noema_controller.py, and a PLAN.md entry — is realistically a 600+ changed-line diff, over the contract's 400-line queue threshold, and cannot be honestly shrunk without splitting the signed-off plan. Queueing rather than splitting unilaterally. Second reason to hold dispatch generally: the last three verifier FAILs (iters 2–4) share one root cause — new files left untracked and vault-file edits living outside the repo never appear in the diff the verifier sees, so even correct work fails verification. Ask: (a) approve splitting 0027 into <=200-line sub-tasks (e.g. operators.py+config / controller+checkpoint / tests+PLAN.md) or authorize the >400-line dispatch as one unit; (b) rule on how workers should evidence new files and vault edits (commit on the loop/* worktree branch before verify vs. git add -N), since IMPLEMENTATION.md currently blocks staging and this is zeroing the trust ledger.
done_when: User records a decision on 0027 (split into sub-tasks vs single authorized dispatch) in the task file or INDEX
User rules on the verifier-evidence convention (commit-before-verify or intent-to-add) so specs can require it
tasks/0027 status updated accordingly (in-progress once dispatched, or blocked-by set to the new sub-task ids)
